transcript.clean_text: the 74th running of the criterium to dofini getting itself off and underway in beautiful conditions today and what a rude awakening at the top of us eight stage race 193 kilometers in total destination beau chastel a big looping course of just over 60 kilometers including two climbs late on but a big climb at the beginning of the day destined to attempt to break away with the king of the mountains jersey being dangled out for the brave well those brave were boue oys and pierre rolong long long in the tooth but high in ambition for a polka dot jersey and some bonus seconds up for grabs as well as if to make a statement team bike exchange working for dylan groenervegan they were keeping the break away on a reasonably tight leash well the bonus seconds that were coming through at the intermediate sprint also counted for an awful lot at the end top five positions up for grabs for the breakaway some splits there were and indeed as soon as gronevegan and some of the other fast men got themselves into trouble then there was a big push out front that walls struggling along with groner vegan they were not alone phil bauhaus also had his troubles for bahrain victorious meanwhile roland was well bossing every single one of the climbs he took maximum points over all of the categorized tests and in so doing he finds himself in the polka dot jersey finding himself in trouble was dylan gronevegan as others decided to bridge over to the breakaway summon gesco rolled the dice and so did a few others as well on that final test the quick men went backwards the gaps were opening up and it was their chances for those who would hit and hope on the descent before the 14 kilometer flat run to home well there were some brave souls out there michael honore was one but he was wound in by a push from india's grenadiers how would they reassemble themselves well it was a callback for everyone for bike exchange to try and bridge the gap that was hovering around the half minute mark with 10 kilometres to go that gap started to extend and finally the fast men knew that their fate was sealed but who would it be of the punchers well there's none stronger than what van art but jesper stuyven had an option ethan hater was also going to be there edward bozenhagan was going to be in the mix as well it was going to be mayhem potentially unless remy cavania could make this stick the french champion from quick step alpha vinyl sped off pippo garner was called to hand to man the pumps the gap was wound in and indeed by the time ethan hater was released we were looking for the belgian tricolore it came through almost center of the road sean quinn racing beautifully starving boxed out of it in the end the best man won while van art delivering himself into the lead of this race a race where every single stage jumbo visma are going to start off with at least one of the day's favorites to take it it's a very good finishing out there benjamin thomas just out of position out of luck this time by had to take the long route home but a long day of 193 kilometers was taken by walt van hart hater in second place ahead of sean quinn paige bosenhagen stuyven venturini van hills benjamin thomas and steinberg your top ten off ten six and four bonus seconds mean that's a gap of four seconds to the good well van out leads this race good work for those who in the breakaway bowie hois and rolon all finding themselves in the top six roland with the climber's jersey the points also owned by one art ethan hater wears white as the best young rider and so to stage two brave our destination a long-ish day slightly shorter than the opener but nonetheless testy to say the least four categorized climbs but it should really be for the sprinters let's see if they have their luck this time by or whether the breakaway spoil their fun slightly lumpy in the rolling let's see how it pans out the dophane is underway the tour de france preparation has begun you
